items.title: Alibaba previews Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4T multimodal model claiming to trail only Fable 5
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team unveiled Qwen3.8-Max-Preview at the World AI Conference in Shanghai on July 19: a 2.4-trillion-parameter sparse Mixture-of-Experts model and the first Qwen release above 1T parameters to natively process images, video, and documents alongside text. Alibaba claims it trails only Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 among frontier models, though it published no benchmark scores and no independent evaluation has verified the claim; open weights are promised "soon" with no date set. The preview is live via Alibaba's Token Plan, Qoder, and QoderWork at 10% of standard pricing.
items.whyItMatters: Another frontier-scale multimodal model just landed at steep early-access pricing — worth a bake-off for cheap document/image-understanding side tasks (like scanning a learner's handwritten French homework) even though Romain and Anna's core voice loop stays on OpenAI Realtime and ElevenLabs.

items.title: Anthropic reinstates Claude Fable 5 for Max/Team Premium, credits-only for Pro/Team Standard
items.summary: Starting July 20, Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back into Max and Team Premium plans at 50% of weekly usage limits, after included access had only been extended through July 19. Pro and Team Standard subscribers keep Fable 5 access solely via usage credits and get a one-time $100 credit. It's the latest swing in an on-again, off-again access pattern Anthropic has run since Fable 5 launched, reportedly driven by compute constraints.
items.whyItMatters: Anthropic's repeated usage-limit reshuffling on its top model is a live signal of how expensive frontier reasoning still is to serve — relevant if the tutor ever leans on Claude for harder tutoring judgments beyond the current OpenAI/ElevenLabs split.

items.title: Perplexity open-sources WANDR, a benchmark exposing how badly research agents fail at scale
items.summary: Perplexity released WANDR (Wide ANd Deep Research) on July 19: an open benchmark and evaluation harness built around 500 realistic research tasks — competitive mapping, due diligence, literature review, market analysis — backed by more than 170,000 source-backed records. Perplexity's own Search-as-Code agent topped the leaderboard but still only reached a 0.363 soft-F1 score; Anthropic placed second at 0.249, and no evaluated system reliably completed large, evidence-backed research tasks.
items.whyItMatters: WANDR's grading method — did the agent find every qualifying fact and cite a page that actually proves it — is a useful template for scoring the tutor's own grammar/dictionary RAG lookups on accuracy rather than just fluency.
